message:
fix(codegen): Aggressive value discard for unknown callables

Changed shouldDiscardValue logic for name calls (closures, callable variables)
to be more aggressive: assume any callable returns a value unless we can prove
it returns void/none.

Previous logic:
- Only discard if type != .none AND type != .unknown
- Closures/PyValue vars with .unknown type were NOT discarded → compile error

New logic:
- Only skip discard if type == .none (proven void)
- All other callables (unknown, PyValue, closures) are discarded

This is safer because:
1. Closures like __m23_c_f generate .call() dispatch returning PyValue
2. Type inference often returns .unknown for generated closure structs
3. Better to over-discard (redundant _ =) than under-discard (compile error)

Result:
- test_bool: Now compiles ✅ (150 tests, 3 failures)
- test_class, test_bytes, test_augassign: Now compile ✅
- ~8 tests moved from compile errors to runtime testing
